name: noble-bedchamber
summary: A noble's private bedchamber in a castle or fortified manor — stone walls, tapestries against the cold, a heavy curtained bed. Grander than a townhouse bedroom, colder too.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle, bed facing the fireplace wall

primary_palette.note: castle chamber — massive fireplace, heavy tapestries blunt the stone cold
warrior noble — sleeps beside their war-kit, ready at any hour
devout noble — bed, desk, altar, spartan by their own choice
hunting-lodge chamber — less formal, trophies instead of tapestries

story_prompts: the lord sleeps behind drawn curtains — the hound on the rug will wake before he does
the chamber has just been vacated at speed — armor half-donned, bed thrown back
a murder has occurred here and the tapestries have been moved to hide a stain
an assignation — the lady is not expecting her husband, who is one corridor away
the old lord died in this bed three generations ago and the family insists nothing has changed

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low warm (0.25-0.4) — fireplace, brazier, candelabra — stone soaks light

anti_patterns: differentiate from the mansion-bedroom — stone not wood, tapestries not paintings, heavier and colder
the bed is fortified — curtains drawn against draft, canopy retains heat
weapons within reach are plausible — this is a noble who expects trouble
tapestries on walls (described/implied), rugs on floor — stone walls and floors both need covering
